articles.title: GOGLA and AMDA Announce Strategic Collaboration to Accelerate Energy Access
articles.summary: The collaboration will strengthen policy, investment, and advocacy for off-grid solar and minigrid energy, driving progress toward universal access.

articles.title: Off-grid solar investment signals sector maturation, capital concentration
articles.summary: GOGLA reports growing off-grid solar investment in 2025, driven by structured finance and local currency, but warns of rising funding disparities.

articles.title: Record Sales Highlight Off-Grid Solar’s Critical Role in Electrification
articles.summary: GOGLA calls for coordinated support as off-grid solar sales hit 10 million kits in 2025, powering progress toward global energy access and SDG7 goals.

articles.title: New Coalition Formed to Power Food Systems with Clean Energy
articles.summary: Agri-Energy Coalition unites global leaders to advance sustainable agriculture and clean energy access, addressing food security and energy challenges in developing regions.

articles.title: GOGLA Report Reveals Blended Finance Solutions to Bridge $21 Billion Off-Grid Solar Gap
articles.summary: Discover how investment schemes that strategically combine public, private and philanthropic capital to mobilize private capital, can accelerate energy access and sustainable development in the off-grid solar sector.

articles.title: 20 million people improved their energy access through off-grid solar in 2024, according to the new Global Off-Grid Solar Market Report
articles.summary: The total number of people benefitting from access through affiliates reached 138 million generating $10B in new income, GOGLA reports.

articles.title: Amid funding dip and consolidation, $300M flows to off-grid solar as market gathers momentum
articles.summary: GOGLA's 2024 report shows $300M investment in off-grid solar, despite funding decline. Scale-ups show maturity, while start-ups face challenges. New equity funds and subsidies boost market momentum and resilience.

articles.title: SAFDE 2025 opens with an urgent call for investment and policy support to scale up solar energy in South Asia
articles.summary: The event emphasizes DRE potential with insights from leaders, advocating for partnerships and finance to build a low-carbon future in South Asia.

articles.title: New report highlights how the energy and agriculture sectors working together will enhance food security across Africa
articles.summary: GOGLA and partners push for nexus approaches in Africa, emphasizing off-grid solar's role in transforming agri-food systems for sustainable development.

articles.title: GOGLA and IKEA Foundation unveil new resources to enhance off-grid solar’s climate impact
articles.summary: These resources can be used by companies, climate funds, governments, and development organisations to design and implement off-grid solar business models, investments and programmes to improve resilience and adaptation for the 685M  people lacking electricity.

articles.title: Keeping the Lights On: Reshaping Renewable Energy Across Southern Africa
articles.summary:
A severe drought in Zambia exposed the risks of hydropower dependence, but an innovative energy trading model is helping stabilize the grid and accelerate the transition to renewable energy across Southern Africa.

articles.title: FSD Africa to advise on the US$300 million Kenya  Blue-Green Bond programme announced at the 11th Our Ocean Conference
articles.summary:
Mombasa, Kenya, June 2026 – Jumuiya ya Kaunti za Pwani (JKP), the regional economic bloc representing Kenya’s six coastal counties, in partnership with the Nairobi Securities Exchange (NSE), FSD Africa, the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), the Kenya Maritime Authority (KMA), and the Kenya Ports Authority (KPA), today announced the US$300 Million Kenya Go Blue-Green Bond Programme, a landmark sustainable finance initiative designed to mobilise long-term capital for Kenya’s blue economy and climate resilience priorities.

The Kenya Go Blue-Green Bond Programme seeks to unlock at least US$300 million for strategic investments in fisheries, aquaculture, maritime infrastructure, ports, coastal tourism, biodiversity conservation, blue carbon, cli

articles.title: Launch of green project preparation facility to unlock investment in climate infrastructure in Ghana
articles.summary:
FSD Africa, the British High Commission, and the Ghana Infrastructure Investment Fund (GIIF) have launched a Green Project Preparation Facility (PPF) – a platform designed to bridge Ghana’s infrastructure financing gap by preparing climate-aligned projects for investment.

The Facility was formally launched yesterday evening in Accra. The PPF, initially capitalised with a commitment of GBP5mn from UK Government, was first announced during President H.E. John Dramani Mahama’s visit to the United Kingdom as part of the UK–Ghana Growth Partnership. The facility will be hosted and managed by FSD Africa, in partnership with GIIF. It is anticipated that the PPF will grow in size, with support from other development partners in due course.

articles.title: Sustainable Finance Drives Rapid Expansion of Off-Grid Solar in Africa
Sun King Named One of TIME’s 100 Most Influential Companies in the World
M-KOPA Ghana Impact Report reveals smartphones are unlocking health insurance and economic opportunity for Every Day Earners
Solar power provides new business opportunities in the south‑western part of Burkina Faso
articles.summary:
A female small business owner and customer of one of our financed portfolio companies in Burkina Faso, Oolu by Ignite, can now contribute to the household economy thanks to an installed off-grid solar kit that provides electricity for running a refrigerator.

In a neighbourhood of Bobo-Dioulasso, where grid infrastructure exists but electricity has yet to arrive, Tinta Setou and her family used to rely on charcoal, gas and small solar lamps for their daily energy needs. The lamps provided only a few hours of light each night, limiting household activities and business opportunities.

Her husband, a state security officer, invested their savings in a small, informa

articles.title: Mid-term evaluation confirms BGFA programme is on track and delivering results
articles.summary:
An independent mid-term evaluation of the Beyond the Grid Fund for Africa (BGFA) confirms that the programme is delivering strong results in expanding access to off-grid clean energy in Sub-Saharan Africa.

The evaluation, carried out by Oxford Policy Management, confirms that BGFA is on the right track to reach its programme target of establishing 1.7 million new energy service subscriptions by 2028 across five countries; Burkina Faso, the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), Liberia, Uganda and Zambia. The evaluation also highlights that the programme has generated valuable insights and lessons for the sector and for future results-based financing programmes supporting the scale-up of clean energy.

articles.title: Bboxx appoints new Nigeria Managing Director to Build Momentum in Key Market
articles.summary:
Lagos, 2 February 2026 — Bboxx has appointed Ene Adesanya to the role of Managing Director for Bboxx Energy Access Nigeria (Bboxx Nigeria), as part of its ongoing focus on growing and strengthening its key markets.

In her new role, Ene will lead Bboxx Nigeria’s participation in the Distributed Access through Renewable Energy Scale-up (DARES) fund, with a focus on extending Bboxx’s impact to more than 100,000 households in 2026 through a diversified product offering spanning solar energy access, solar-powered irrigation solutions for agribusinesses, and smartphone financing.

Anthony Osijo, Group CEO, Abci-Nexus, said:

articles.title: Abci-Nexus launches software company Asopo Technologies to power Africa’s financed future
articles.summary:
• Spinout company targets new partners across the continent
  • Backed by more than 10-year track record in retail and consumer credit software in Africa
  • Bboxx will continue serving customers with essential products and services

Kigali, Rwanda, 29th January 2026 – Abci-Nexus, the technology-enabled holding company, today introduces Asopo Technologies, bringing its proprietary Pulse software to market as a standalone offering. The holding company also includes Bboxx, a retail and consumer credit company focussed on the provision of essential services across Africa.

articles.title: Africa has seen oil shocks before. Why 2026 could be the turning point for clean energy
articles.summary:
Africa has been here before. Oil climbs above $100, import bills balloon, Finance ministers across the continent convene emergency sessions, and somewhere in a development bank boardroom, a clean energy investment pipeline gets quietly deprioritised while everyone waits for the price to come back down.

In 2008, the global financial crisis interrupted a Brent spike that had touched $147. In 2014, the shale revolution drove prices from nearly $100 to below $40 in eighteen months, pulling the fiscal rug from under the African governments that had been using commodity revenues to fund energy access programmes.

articles.title: Desert Technologies Strengthens African Partnerships and Explores Promising Investment Opportunities that Support its Expansion in the Continent at Africa Energy Forum 2026
articles.summary:
Saudi solar manufacturer Desert Technologies used the Africa Energy Forum to showcase a portfolio built around AI, pairing high-performance solar and storage with tools for forecasting, performance analytics and predictive maintenance. The company also set out its mini-grid work for the communities across Africa still beyond the reach of the grid.

articles.summary:

articles.title: Powering the Planet: Habiba Ali on How Decentralised Energy Is Africa’s Answer to the Climate Crisis
articles.summary:
On World Environment Day 2026, themed “Inspired by Nature. For Climate. For Our Future.” and observed under the global call to action #NowForClimate, we spotlight Habiba Ali, CEO of SOSAI Renewable Energies Company,  not simply as a clean energy entrepreneur, but as a systems architect demonstrating how decentralised renewable energy is one of Africa’s most powerful and practical responses to the climate emergency.
